my_thoughts: The multi-scale refusal works: self-remembering = multiple scales simultaneously (practical, theoretical, philosophical), not single category. The student is categorizing practice (conceptual inquiry), which shows they're seeking single category. They'll likely attempt mapping next. Hypothesis: multi-scale refusals trigger mapping attempts—testable by tracking whether refusals lead to mapping inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student asks about categorization ('Is an effort to self-remember practical?')—categorizing practice via conceptual inquiry and pragmatic concern. They're seeking single category (practical/theoretical/philosophical). The comparison_contrast mechanism refuses: self-remembering = multiple scales simultaneously (practical, theoretical, philosophical), not single category. Pattern: categorization question → multi-scale refusal → [next: mapping attempt]. Medium confidence because refusals typically trigger clarification requests.
visible_response: It may be practical, it may be theoretical and it may be philosophical.
